title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a history-forensics evidence packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Blame Context evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Blame Context assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Blame Context has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Blame Context precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The author shown by blame necessarily introduced the current bug.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Blame Context case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Blame Context case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Blame Context case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Blame Context case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-blame-context-1okj9p" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Blame Context into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-blame-context-1okj9p
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Blame Context structural check is only one gate. a history-forensics evidence packet must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Blame Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
steps.why: Retrieving Blame Context through the concrete a history-forensics evidence packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
